_description: Named risk-profile overrides for config.json — the four-tier risk LADDER, applied by /set-risk-profile. See docs/risk-profiles.md for the full design rationale (what the ladder's axis is, why its thresholds are profile-relative, and why trade count is deliberately not a ladder dimension), and GATES.md for the gate-by-gate catalog.
_ladder_note: The ladder is the ONLY profile registry now. Its axis is RISKIER TRADES, not more trades: climbing a rung loosens entry quality (IV floor, credit floor, short-call delta, OTM distance) and is offset by fewer concurrent positions and a tighter stop. Trade count is therefore flat across rungs (daily_ic_trade_target = 2 everywhere, always <= max_concurrent_ics). Thresholds that used to be shared absolutes are now derived per profile from its own min_iv_rank and credit floor (low_iv_credit_floor_iv_rank_offset, low_iv_credit_relief_multiple, late_entry_bias_iv_rank_offset in config.json) — a shared absolute silently flattened the ladder, see docs/risk-profiles.md. In reporting, one PORTFOLIO is formed per (profile x symbol) pair, each with its own max_concurrent_ics and daily-target budget.
_removed_note: The 15 symbol/wing/credit experiment cells (small-/medium-/large-/explore-) were removed on 2026-07-18 pending a rethink; they are recoverable from git history. They pinned a symbol and wing per cell, which conflicted with the (profile x symbol) portfolio model where the symbol is its own axis. Re-introduce them as symbol-agnostic branches if the study resumes.

conservative._note: Default: today's settings. Strict IV/credit floors, wide OTM buffers, latest entry time (12:00 PM). Fewest trades (~1–2/day), highest per-trade safety margin. Best for learning, uncertain markets, or recovery after losses.

moderate._note: Tier 1: Relax IV-rank (0.22) and credit floors (0.12), enter earlier (11:00 AM), tighten stop to 0.93 to offset. ~1 more trade/day vs conservative, thinner per-trade credit margin but paired with tighter stop. Use after 2–4 weeks on conservative if entry rejections are high.

aggressive._note: Tier 2: Tier 1 + accept tighter OTM strikes (delta 0.22, calls 0.30% OTM, puts 0.25% OTM); offset with max_concurrent_ics=3 (25% fewer positions) and stop_trigger_ratio=0.90 (5% tighter than conservative). ~2–3 more trades/week, each one riskier but total exposure capped. Requires active stop management. Use after 2+ weeks on moderate with 60%+ win rate.

very-aggressive._note: Tier 3: Tier 2 + regime gates relaxed (VIX pause at 30, ATR pause at 40); trade through higher-vol/trending conditions. Tightest offsets: max_concurrent_ics=2 (50% fewer), stop_trigger_ratio=0.85 (10% tighter than conservative). Highest activity (~3–5 more trades/week on active weeks), highest per-trade risk. FOR DELIBERATE SHORT EXPERIMENTS ONLY — test high-VIX/ATR tactics for 1 week with explicit drawdown limits. Not a permanent trading mode.
